title: Substance Addiction (Compulsory Assessment and Treatment) Act 2017

sections.title: Criteria for compulsory assessment
sections.content: A person may be subject to compulsory assessment if they have a severe substance addiction, have seriously diminished capacity to make decisions about substance use, and pose a serious danger to their health or safety or the safety of others.

sections.title: Police powers
sections.content: A constable who believes a person meets the criteria may apprehend the person and take them to a health facility for assessment. The person must be assessed by a medical practitioner within 24 hours.
